q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is md5(password.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest(), a CPU-only cryptographic hash computation with no network, shell, or irreversible side effects.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: Function returns a boolean equality check (self.pwd_hash == md5(...)). The return value does not expose which hashing algorithm was used.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: Not evaluated because Q2 is false.

mock_guidance.target: Mock the md5 function or hashlib.md5 constructor.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the input argument (the encoded password bytes) passed to md5 to verify the sensitive data flow.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a mock object with a hexdigest() method that returns a predictable string (e.g., matching self.pwd_hash) to allow execution to continue.
notes: Recording this call is necessary because the boolean return value obscures the use of the weak MD5 algorithm.
Asserting that md5 was called directly proves the weak-sensitive-data-hashing violation.
